visual_notes: Screenshots based on QA28 visual baseline (same codebase, ~1h prior). Chromium unavailable in this environment due to pthread thread-creation limit. Key visual observations: office floor renders correctly on desktop; mobile has ~40% dead space below the floor; window element renders as large opaque block on mobile right side.

suggestions.title: workers.json is a flat array — real worker data NEVER loads
suggestions.description: The live GitHub workers.json is a raw JSON array [{worker1}, {worker2}], but App.jsx reads data.workers ?? [] and data.roster ?? []. Accessing .workers on a JS array returns undefined, so both resolve to []. Result: allWorkers is always empty regardless of server data, all characters are permanently rendered as DEFAULT_ROSTER ghosts, and the Dashboard always shows an empty worker grid. This is almost certainly the root cause of the persistent DEFAULT_ROSTER id mismatch (flagged 9x) — real data never arrives. Fix: wrap the JSON as { workers: [...], roster: [...] } in workers.json, OR change App.jsx to handle a flat array: Array.isArray(data) ? data : (data.workers ?? []).

suggestions.title: Keyboard shortcut hash uses literal # prefix — double-hash breaks tab sync
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 565-566 set window.location.hash = '#office' and '#dashboard' — both include a literal # in the value. Setting window.location.hash = '#office' produces the URL fragment ##office (browser prepends # to the assigned value). When handleHashChange reads window.location.hash.slice(1), it gets '#office' instead of 'office', matching neither branch, so the tab state never updates on keyboard shortcut. Tab buttons on lines 787/800 correctly use window.location.hash = 'office' (no # prefix). Fix: remove the # prefix from both keyboard shortcut lines (565 and 566).

suggestions.title: StatsBar stuck-worker detection silently fails — DEFAULT_ROSTER workers have no 'started' field
suggestions.description: StatsBar line 327 filters stuck workers with (Date.now() - new Date(w.started).getTime()) > 7_200_000. DEFAULT_ROSTER workers have no started field, so w.started is undefined. new Date(undefined).getTime() returns NaN, and Date.now() - NaN is NaN, which is always false in comparisons. The stuck-worker badge never fires for roster-based workers. Since real workers never load (bug #1), the started field from fetched workers is also never exercised in production. Fix: use w.updated_at ?? w.started as the timestamp source and add a null guard before the subtraction.

suggestions.title: Luna's SVG linearGradient ID is always 'luna-hair-luna' — DOM id collision risk
suggestions.description: In CharacterAvatar (Office.jsx), Luna's gradient is const gradId = `luna-hair-${workerId}` where workerId is always 'luna', always producing 'luna-hair-luna'. SVG gradient IDs must be globally unique in the document. If the Luna character SVG is ever rendered in multiple places simultaneously, duplicate IDs cause browsers to silently apply the first definition to all instances, breaking the gradient for the second render. Fix: generate a unique suffix per instance using React 18's useId() hook, or a module-level incrementing counter.

suggestions.title: Tab switching via window.location.hash pollutes browser history
suggestions.description: Every tab click sets window.location.hash = 'office' or 'dashboard', pushing a new browser history entry each time. After navigating Office -> Dashboard -> Office -> Dashboard, pressing back cycles through internal tab states instead of returning to the previous page. Fix: replace all window.location.hash = tab assignments (lines 565, 566, 509, 510, 787, 800) with history.replaceState(null, '', '#' + tab) to update the URL without creating history entries.

suggestions.title: Mobile: ~40% empty viewport below office floor
suggestions.description: On 375px mobile, the office floor's aspect-ratio: 4/3 renders at ~250px tall. After header (~100px), tabs (~50px), and the floor (~280px), roughly 300px of empty vertical space sits before the footer. The office tab has zero secondary content on mobile. Fix: add a compact vibe summary strip or mini stats panel below the office floor on mobile, or adjust aspect-ratio to 1/1 at max-width 480px via a media query.

suggestions.title: stats-bar hardcoded 5-column grid breaks when Error or Stuck cards conditionally appear
suggestions.description: The stats-bar CSS uses grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr), creating exactly 5 columns. When the Error card or Stuck worker card conditionally renders (making 6 or 7 total cards), overflow cards spill onto a second row. A lone 6th card stretches to fill 1/5 of the row width while the other 4/5 is empty — visually broken. Fix: replace repeat(5, 1fr) with repeat(auto-fill, minmax(130px, 1fr)) so the grid adapts naturally to variable card counts.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar milestone flash fires spuriously on initial mount for already-progressed workers
suggestions.description: ProgressBar's useEffect iterates MILESTONES [25, 50, 75, 100] and triggers a CSS flash animation for any milestone where progress >= m AND the milestone is not in milestonesRef.current. On first mount, milestonesRef is an empty Set, so a worker already at 60% immediately triggers the 25% and 50% milestone animations — an unwanted celebratory pulse on every page load or component remount. Fix: pre-populate milestonesRef.current on initialization with milestones already passed: milestonesRef.current = new Set(MILESTONES.filter(m => m <= progress)).

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime utility is duplicated in App.jsx and Office.jsx
suggestions.description: getRelativeTime is defined as a standalone function in App.jsx (lines 26-35) and then reimplemented inline inside the Character component in Office.jsx (lines 381-390). Two nearly-identical implementations mean any bug fix or format change must be applied twice. Fix: extract to src/utils/time.js and import in both files.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard shows generic role emoji; Office floor shows custom SVG art — identity inconsistency
suggestions.description: WorkerCard (App.jsx line 245) uses getRoleEmoji(worker.role) — the same emoji for ALL workers with the same role (e.g., every QA Engineer gets the same magnifying glass). The Office floor uses the rich custom CharacterAvatar SVG with unique per-person illustrations. Users clicking a character in the office land on the Dashboard and see a different, generic representation of the same person. Fix: import CharacterAvatar into App.jsx and use it in WorkerCard so both views share the same character identity.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard, ActivityLog, StatsBar, ProgressBar still not memoized (flagged 7x)
suggestions.description: None of these heavy components are wrapped in React.memo. Every 30-second syncFromGitHub() call triggers a full re-render of all WorkerCards (flip state, progress milestone effect, activity history filtering), the entire ActivityLog with time-group sorting, and StatsBar with stuck-worker useMemo. Even when data is unchanged, all children re-render from scratch. Fix: wrap WorkerCard, StatsBar, ActivityLog, ActivityLogEntry, and ProgressBar in React.memo.

suggestions.title: No ErrorBoundary — any render crash shows a blank white screen with no recovery
suggestions.description: Neither App.jsx nor Office.jsx wraps children in a React ErrorBoundary. A thrown exception in any child component (malformed worker data, null dereference in CharacterAvatar, bad timestamp in formatDuration) crashes the entire React tree with a blank white page and no recovery path. Fix: add a top-level ErrorBoundary in main.jsx and a secondary one around the Office component to isolate office-floor render crashes from the rest of the dashboard.

suggestions.title: Footer version hardcoded as 'v1.0.0-chaos' string literal
suggestions.description: The footer hardcodes v1.0.0-chaos as a string (App.jsx line 920). This silently falls out of date as the project evolves. Fix: import { version } from '../package.json' (Vite supports JSON imports by default) and render version dynamically so the footer always reflects the real package.json version.
